---
title: Separable Equations
description: Differential equations that can be separated and integrated.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Recognize separable equations.
- Separate variables conceptually.
- Connect solving to integration.
:::

A separable equation can be rearranged so all `y` terms are on one side and all
`x` terms are on the other.

::: {.practice-box}
1. Is `dy/dx = x*y` separable?
2. What operation is used after separating variables?
3. Why does a constant of integration appear?
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: yes.

It can be rearranged as `(1/y) dy = x dx`, assuming `y` is not zero.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: integration.

After separating variables, integrate both sides.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: indefinite integration creates a family of solutions, and the constant
selects one member of that family.
:::
